paper_title: Serial Section Analysis of Central Spindles

methods.description: This method involves analyzing central spindles from cells at different stages of mitosis by tracking microtubules (MTs) across serial sections. The purpose is to understand the structure and changes in the spindle during mitosis.

steps.description: Central spindles from five cells were analyzed: one in metaphase, three in anaphase, and one in telophase.
steps.searchable_summary: select central spindles from cells at different mitosis stages

steps.description: In cross-section, the stages of mitosis were determined by the following criteria, derived from a previous paper.
steps.searchable_summary: determine mitosis stages using cross-section criteria

steps.description: For tracking MTs, each negative of a cross section was printed to a final magnification of approximately • 150,000.
The print of each cross section was numbered, and the set was arranged to correspond with the sequence of the sections.
steps.searchable_summary: number and arrange cross-section prints sequentially

steps.description: In a preliminary analysis of the central spindle, we counted the number of tubules in each section.
For a more thorough analysis of spindle structure, we tracked each central spindle MT from the section where it began to the section where it ended.
steps.searchable_summary: track each MT from start to end section

steps.description: This was accomplished by placing a sheet of clear acetate over the first print of a given series and marking the position of every MT with a fine-tipped pen.
steps.searchable_summary: mark MT positions on acetate over prints

steps.description: Each 'mark' was a code which allowed each MT to be identified separately using a two-element code of a letter plus a single digit.
steps.searchable_summary: assign unique codes to each MT

steps.description: When all the MTs on the first print were marked, the fact that they were present in that section was recorded on graph paper.
steps.searchable_summary: record MT presence on graph paper

steps.description: The acetate sheet from the first print was then superimposed upon the print of the adjacent serial section to track continuous MTs.
steps.searchable_summary: superimpose acetate to track continuous MTs

steps.description: All new MTs appearing in that section were given a new code.
steps.searchable_summary: assign new codes to new MTs

steps.description: Any MT that was present in the previous section but not in the next was recorded as having ended in that section.
steps.searchable_summary: record MTs that end in a section

steps.description: The overlay from the second print was then put in register with the print of the next section and so on until all the prints were processed.
steps.searchable_summary: continue overlay process for all sections

steps.description: The product of such an analysis is a record of the total length of each MT measured in number of sections and a one-dimensional record of the position of the tubule ends.
steps.searchable_summary: compile MT length and position records
